name: Sinsera Installer (bundled images → NVMe / USB)
description: DietPi image that bundles every other Sinsera image (vanilla, kiosk, claude-cli-pi, raspyjack, flipper) on its rootfs and provides a `flash-to-nvme` command that lets you write any of them to an attached NVMe or USB drive after booting. The intended workflow: flash this onto an SD card, boot the Pi from SD, run `flash-to-nvme <image>`, configure Pi 5 boot order for NVMe-first, power off, remove SD, boot from NVMe.

preconfigured.install_tool: /usr/local/bin/flash-to-nvme (interactive, lists bundled images, prompts for target disk, writes with progress, sets Pi 5 BOOT_ORDER)

first_boot_sequence: DietPi finishes its standard first-boot setup
Automation_Custom_Script.sh runs — moves the bundled .img.xz files from /opt/ark-extras/ into /opt/sinsera-images/, sets up the flash-to-nvme tool, prints the welcome MOTD
SSH in as root (key-based, no password)
Plug an NVMe (via HAT) or USB-attached drive in
Run `flash-to-nvme` — interactive picker for which image + which target disk
Set Pi 5 BOOT_ORDER (the tool does this for you), power off, remove SD, boot from NVMe
authorization_notice: Same authorization scope as the bundled images. Use only on hardware you own.
